items.headline: City launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a business license amnesty program targeting shopping center operators, likely to help bring unlicensed or non-compliant businesses into the system. This could affect local compliance and revenue while giving struggling small businesses a chance to get current with the city.

items.headline: New economic incentives coming for retail and startup businesses
items.summary: The city council approved economic development incentives designed to attract and retain retail stores and startup companies in Cupertino. This signals the city is trying to diversify its economy and address retail challenges in local shopping areas.

items.headline: New mixed-use development approved for East Middlefield Road
items.summary: The City Council advanced a development agreement for 490 East Middlefield Road, a mixed-use residential project that will change how that area looks and functions. This was the second reading, meaning the project is moving toward final approval.

items.headline: Mountain View applying for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application to California's Prohousing Incentive Program (Round 4) to secure state funding for housing initiatives. This could bring additional resources to support local housing development.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking initiative. This program typically addresses vehicle dwelling and parking for unhoused residents.

items.headline: City planning major changes around Santa Clara Station
items.summary: The Station Area Task Force is reviewing a new land use plan and design guidelines for the area around Santa Clara Station. This will shape what gets built there over the next decade—housing, transit access, and the character of that neighborhood.
